string.text: You stand with your Master Herald. The household awaits your word — which matters concern you?
Step away from the Master Herald.
Return to Clan Home to perform this action.
The body stirs, my lord. Your heralds stand ready at the heart.
Tell me what you can do for the clan.

Five offices serve this house. Mine is the Master — your voice and theirs to you. Four others wait for your appointment: an Exchequer for the coin, a Watch for the roads, a Muster for the levy, and an Arms for the drill. Each is yours to fill or leave empty as you see fit.
At your service, my lord. The household waits.
Show me the full ledger of actions.
I want to appoint a Herald.
A Herald has served their time. I want to dismiss one.
Exchequer Herald — steward of the coin.
Watch Herald — eyes on the roads.
Muster Herald — caller of the levy.
Arms Herald — master of the drill.
Who shall I name to this office?
No one suits the office today.
It is done. They will take their station within the day.
As you say. The office stands empty.
Your household has taken root in {SETTLEMENT}. Visit it to meet your Master Herald.
The heart has been taken, but the house is not dead. Name us a new seat, and we will beat again.
1 herald released from office. Establish a new Clan Home in any owned town or castle.
{COUNT} heralds released from office. Establish a new Clan Home in any owned town or castle.
Your {OFFICE} Herald, {HERO}, has been taken prisoner by {CAPTOR}.
Your {OFFICE} Herald, {HERO}, has returned to office.
Your {OFFICE} Herald, {HERO}, has died ({DETAIL}). The office stands empty.
Watch Herald: a {FACTION} party ({PARTY}) is moving within reach of {SETTLEMENT}.
No worthy target available. Declare war first, or wait for an enemy clan to rise.
Word has spread of your attempt. The whole faction holds it against you.
[Hearth & Herald] Cannot tear down: no active campaign.
[Hearth & Herald] Tear-down complete. Save the game, then you may uninstall the mod safely.
A veteran {TROOP} has proven themselves and could be ennobled as a companion — speak with your Herald.
No veterans have yet proven themselves enough to be ennobled, my lord.
Choose veterans to ennoble as companions. Each joins your clan with the culture, skills, and arms of their service — one is taken from the ranks.
A veteran {TROOP} is ennobled — {NAME} joins your companions.
That soldier is no longer in your party.
The household stands ready. Which matters concern you, my lord?
Let us turn to other matters.
Every herald office is already filled.
Administrative — clan records, council, and the seat of power.
Appointments — assign, dismiss, and re-assign Heralds.
Intrigue — covert work against your enemies.
Military — muster, drills, defences, and patrols.
Settlement Support — loyalty, militia, food, unrest, and revenue.
Specialist offices — call on a Herald's particular craft.
Status & Reports — bonuses, warnings, and summaries.
Your Heralds convene in council — all is well across the house.
Your Heralds convene in council — {COUNT} matter(s) await your eye.
Which office's herald shall be released?
Enable household members in the mod options (Family & Clan Continuity).
Enable pregnancy & heirs in the mod options (Family & Clan Continuity).
Enable romance & marriage in the mod options (Family & Clan Continuity).
Field Report — where they are and how they fare
Letter of Reproach — a sharp correction, and favour to a notable
Let us see to the herald offices.
What shall your herald send to {NAME}?
To which companion shall your herald send word?
No office is available for that.
No clan home is established yet.
No vacant office to move them to.
No herald holds an office to dismiss.
No herald holds an office to re-assign.
No herald holds an office to suspend.
The Exchequer — income and the ledgers.
The Watch — scouting and early warning.
It is done. They take their station within the day.
Who shall I name to this office?
The {CH_OFFICE_NAME} office {CH_OFFICE_STATE}. What is your will?
It is done. The office stands empty.
No one suits the office today.
Let us turn to other matters.
Which office concerns you, my lord?
Let us turn to the business of the household.
Move which herald from their current office?
Active offices are suspended; suspended offices resume.
Coin turns captives to colours. Each chosen brigand joins your household bearing their old gear and harder ways — up to {MAX} this visit.
A {TROOP} of {PLACE} takes your colours — {NAME} joins your household{HINT}.
No eligible companion or family member for {OFFICE} Herald. Heroes who govern a settlement, lead their own party, or run a caravan cannot take office until freed of that duty.
The {OFFICE} office is suspended; {HERO} stands down for now.
{LABEL} is not yet within my power, my lord — that will come in time.
That needs your hand in person, my lord — or a clan of Tier {TIER} to direct it from afar.
Not yet, my lord. Give it one more day.
Not yet, my lord. Give it {DAYS} more days.
Return to Clan Home ({HOME}) to consult the Master Herald in person.
It cannot be done, my lord: {REASON}.
Show me the state of the offices.
Send word after our missing heralds.
We will move the seat of the clan.
Send a messenger to a companion.
How fare my family and companions?
Which veterans are ready to be ennobled?
Take some local folk into our service.
Turn captured brigands to our colours.
Help one of our house court another.
Arrange a marriage for our house.
Pair a mentor and a student of our house.
A couple of our house would try for an heir.
Send a gift to strengthen a marriage.
Welcome a household member to our hearth.
Send a gift to a household member.
A household member would bear a child.
There are lords without a banner — let us offer them ours.
Offer a Captive Lord Our Banner
A captive lord sits in our cells — offer them a place.
Let us warm relations with a worthy house.
See to the defence of our villages.
Call the muster through the household.
Lift coin from an enemy's coffers.
Appoint a Herald to an Office
Re-assign a Herald to Another Office
Suspend an office for a time.
Watch — give me a threat briefing.
How does a herald's skill weigh in?
All is well, my lord. The council has no warnings.
Garrisons hold {GARRISON} across {FIEFS} seat(s); militia number {MILITIA}.
No seats to garrison — the host rides with you.
No Arms serves — the drills go unled.
Arms — the readiness of the host:
House {NAME}: Tier {TIER} · {GOLD} denars · {ACTIVE} of 4 specialist offices serving.
The books turn {NET} denars a day.
No Exchequer serves — the ledgers go unaudited.
Exchequer — the coin and the ledgers:
{ACTIVE} of 4 specialist offices serving · {KIN} kin, {COMPANIONS} companion(s).
The seat of the house is {PLACE}.
Master Herald — the state of the house:
…and {COUNT} more. Summon the Herald Council for the full report.
No Muster serves — recruitment runs unaided.
{PARTIES} party(ies) afield with {TROOPS} under arms · {COMPANIONS} companion(s) in service.
Muster — the manpower of the house:
No clan to convene a council for, my lord.
No warnings abroad. Summon the Herald Council in person for the full report.
Summon the Herald Council for the full report.
No active wars — the roads are quiet.
No Watch serves — warnings will come late.
{NAME} drills the garrison of {PLACE} without mercy (Tactics {TACTICS}) — {COUNT} body(ies) of troops sharpened, and the watch stands the readier for it (security {SECURITY}).
The ledgers of {PLACE} are set straight — prosperity {PROSPERITY}.
A banquet in {NAME}'s honour warms relations{HINT} — now {REL}.
The larder of {PLACE} is laid in against a siege{HINT} — food {FOOD}, security {SECURITY}.
The elite muster of {PLACE} answers your Muster Herald — {COUNT} {TROOP} take your colours.
Grain is brought into {PLACE} — food stores {AMOUNT}.
The garrison of {PLACE} is put through hard drills{HINT} — {COUNT} body(ies) of troops better trained for the field.
The garrison of {PLACE} is inspected and reinforced{HINT} — {COUNT} fresh {TROOP} take the wall (security {SECURITY}).
A fitting gift wants 1000 denars, my lord.
Governors coordinated across {COUNT} holding(s) — loyalty improved.
{COUNT} office(s) accounted for and cleared for fresh appointment.
The household's affairs are set in good order.
The household rallies to {PLACE}'s support — loyalty {LOYALTY}.
Every denar is accounted for, my lord — the ledger is impeccable.
The books turn {NET} denars a day.
The house holds no towns or castles to reckon.
The ledger is reckoned, my lord; a sharper hand would find more still.
{COUNT} seat(s) hold {PROSPERITY} prosperity and {FOOD} in stores.
Heralds work the streets of {PLACE} — loyalty rises (now {LOYALTY}).
Militia support is disabled by your compatibility settings.
The muster is called through the household{HINT} — {COUNT} fresh recruit(s) take your colours across the realm.
No Arms Herald serves to lead so hard a drill — appoint one first, my lord.
No clan whose books to keep, my lord.
No companions to send word to.
No local folk answered the call to arms.
There is no garrison at {PLACE} to drill, my lord.
There is no garrison nor host to receive the levy.
{PLACE} keeps no standing garrison to inspect just now.
{PLACE} has no garrison to march reinforcements into.
There is no host nor garrison to muster into.
No levy of {PLACE}'s stock can be marched to its walls.
No levy of {PLACE}'s stock answers the muster.
No lord of standing will receive our envoy just now.
No missing or fallen heralds to account for, my lord.
There is no Muster Herald to raise the elite levy, my lord — appoint one first.
No clan parties are afield to recall.
No clan seat at which to raise the levy.
No clan settlement to attend to.
No clan settlement to audit, my lord.
No clan settlement with a garrison.
No veteran levy could be raised here.
No clan village stands in need of defending.
Not enough influence, my lord — this needs {AMOUNT}.
The {OFFICE} office stands empty, my lord — appoint a herald first.
Only heroes hold {PLACE} — there are no rank troops to drill.
Patrols ride the marches{HINT} — {COUNT} holding(s) on watch; no hostiles abroad.
Patrols bring word{HINT} — {COUNT} hostile party(ies) sighted near our {ALERTED} holding(s).
Reinforcements march to {PLACE}{HINT} — {COUNT} {TROOP} bolster the garrison (security {SECURITY}).
The messenger returns with word of {NAME}.
No enemy forces stir near them.
Hostiles abroad near them — {COUNT} enemy force(s) within reach.
{NAME} already chafes under your hand, my lord — a reproach now and they may forsake us. I will not send it.
A messenger carries your correction to {NAME} and your favour to {NOTABLE} — {NAME} smarts (relation {NAME_REL}), {NOTABLE} is flattered (relation {NOTABLE_REL}).
There is no notable of standing near {NAME} to court with this — best left unsent.
The seat of the clan is now {PLACE}.
The seat could not be moved.
Stand within the town or castle you would make your seat, my lord.
Grievances are heard and the people of {PLACE} are calmed{HINT} — loyalty {LOYALTY}.
The village of {PLACE} is shored up{HINT} — hearth {HEARTH}, militia {MILITIA}.
Which wedded woman of your house shall try for a child?
{NAME} did not conceive this time — try again later.
No wedded woman of your house can bear a child just now.
No one suitable could be welcomed to the household here.
Which household member shall bear a child?
No household member can bear a child just now.
You keep no household members to gift.
Which of your house shall begin a courtship?
None nearby are suitable for {NAME} to court.
No adult clan members to begin a courtship, my lord.
You keep no household members to part with.
{NAME} departs the household with your leave.
The gift could not be sent.
{NAME} and {TARGET} are not ready to wed (court them first).
Which of your house shall be wed?
No eligible match for {NAME} is ready to wed.
No unwed adult of your house to arrange a marriage for.
Choose the mentor (their strongest craft is taught).
You need at least two adults of the house to pair a mentor and student.
The household keeps no record yet.
{STUDENT} will study under {MENTOR} — skill will pass between them each day.
Whose marriage shall a gift strengthen?
No wedded member of your house to send a gift.
{NAME} rides at your side, my lord — speak with them yourself.
{NAME} keeps no party afield, my lord — a messenger would have nowhere to ride.
There is no one of that name left to send word to, my lord.
{NAME} sits in a cell, my lord — no messenger may reach them there.
The household keeps no record of veterans yet.
All is well, my lord. No warnings abroad.
No towns or castles to report on.
No specialist offices are active — appoint heralds to gain advantages.
No active wars — the roads are quiet, my lord.
No Watch Herald active — intrigue is unavailable until one is appointed.
the holder leads their own party
{HERO} takes up residence at {SETTLEMENT} as {OFFICE} Herald.
{HERO} sets out for {SETTLEMENT} to take up the {OFFICE} post, arriving in about a day.
{HERO} sets out for {SETTLEMENT} to take up the {OFFICE} post, arriving in about {DAYS} days.
{HERO} has reached {SETTLEMENT} and taken up the {OFFICE} post.
{HERO}'s journey to the {OFFICE} post is called off.
{HERO} is released from the {OFFICE} office and returns to your party.
{HERO} is released from the {OFFICE} office and remains at {SETTLEMENT}.
Your heralds have taken up residence at {SETTLEMENT}. Appointed heralds now serve from your Clan Home.
Bid me review the offices, settlements, or warnings for the detail.
{ACTIVE} of 4 specialist offices are filled and serving.
The house holds {FIEFS} seat(s) and fields {PARTIES} party(ies).
Each office is sharpened by a primary skill:
Higher skill scales the office's effect toward its cap.
— How a Herald's Skill Weighs In —
(No Watch Herald active — the briefing is limited.)
{NAME} accepts your banner over the cell{HINT}.
A captive lord may be freed to our service. Whom shall we offer?
Offer a Captive Lord Our Banner
That captive is no longer ours to offer a place.
{NAME} forsakes the lost cause and takes your banner{HINT}.
That lord is beyond our reach now.
We make no such offers to captives today.
No captive lord sits in our cells worth the offer.
We send no envoys to rebel lords today.
No landless rebel lord will hear our envoy, my lord.
A lord without a banner may take ours. Whom shall we approach?
The Ransom Broker keeps no counter today.
Not enough coin — the broker wants {COST} denars.
That captive is no longer yours to ransom.
Not enough coin — a hand costs {COST} denars.
Not enough coin, my lord — this needs {AMOUNT} denars.
No brigands among your prisoners worth the broker's coin.
You have no clan to take folk into.
No local folk are willing to take service.
No one suitable could be found to take service.
No brigand could be turned this time.
Your clan does not own this settlement.
Only towns and castles can serve as a Clan Home.
